t-scene1-intro: Welcome to Ballycrann. I'm Mary, your guide. We're standing at the harbour, boats clinking in the breeze. To walk east along the high street, swipe right with one finger, anywhere on the screen.
t-scene1-vignette-1: Past the bakery, the smell of warm soda bread on the air.
t-scene1-vignette-2: Along the stone wall, ivy spilling over the top.
t-scene1-vignette-3: You've reached the post office, with its bright red door.
t-scene3-intro: Now, to walk back west, swipe left.
t-scene3-vignette-1: Past the stone wall again, cobbles uneven underfoot.
t-scene3-vignette-2: The bakery — Eileen waves from the doorway.
t-scene3-vignette-3: Home to the harbour. You can hear the rigging clinking.
t-scene4-intro: To look up — at the spire, or the gulls overhead — swipe up.
t-scene4-vignette-1: The church spire, a single rook circling above.
t-scene4-vignette-2: A patch of blue between two chimneys.
t-scene4-vignette-3: Three gulls cutting across the cloud.
t-scene5-intro: And to look down at the ground, swipe down.
t-scene5-vignette-1: Smooth cobbles, worn by a hundred years of feet.
t-scene5-vignette-2: A copper penny — heads up, lucky for some.
t-scene5-vignette-3: A sea-smoothed stone, shaped like a small heart.
t-scene6-intro: Last one. When you find someone you'd like to greet, you double tap. Two quick taps with one finger.
t-scene6-vignette-1: Mary at the corner shop. Lovely day, she says.
t-scene6-vignette-2: Old Tom on the bench. He tips his cap to you.
t-scene6-vignette-3: Eileen the baker, waving from her doorway.
t-scene7-intro: Beautifully done. Now let's put it together. Pat's gone and lost his dog. Swipe right twice to walk down the lane, then listen carefully.
t-mini-stage1: Down the lane, past the holly hedge. Once more, swipe right.
t-mini-stage2: Past the iron gate, into the field. Listen carefully…
t-mini-bark-cue: There — you can hear him. Double tap to call him over.
t-mini-almost-greet: Almost — double tap to greet the dog.
t-complete: Beautifully done. The tutorial is complete. You're now at the harbour in Ballycrann. Swipe right to walk east through the village, swipe left to walk back. Swipe up or down to look around. Double tap to say hello to anyone you meet.
almost-double-tap: Almost — two quick taps with one finger.
w-intro: Back in Ballycrann, at the harbour. Swipe right to walk east through the village, swipe left to walk back. Swipe up or down to look around. Double tap to say hello to anyone you meet.
w-edge-west: You're at the harbour. The road ends here. No further west.
w-edge-east: The road bends out of Ballycrann. Just fields and sheep beyond. Maybe another day.
w-nobody: Nobody here to greet just now.
w-harbour-desc: You're at the harbour. Boats clinking in the breeze.
w-harbour-up: Gulls wheel against the cloud. One drops a clam onto the stones.
w-harbour-down: Wet cobbles, a length of frayed rope, a fishing net coiled by a bollard.
w-bakery-desc: Past the bakery. The smell of warm soda bread on the air.
w-bakery-up: A wisp of smoke from the chimney. A pigeon dozing on the slate.
w-bakery-down: Flour on the doorstep. Boot prints in it from earlier.
w-bridie-desc: Bridie McGregor's doorway. She's there with a basket of eggs.
w-bridie-up: A trellis of late roses on the wall. A robin in the hedge.
w-bridie-down: Slate paving stones. A cat on the step watching you.
w-bridie-greet-1: Bridie smiles. "A grand morning. Mind yourself on the cobbles."
w-bridie-greet-2: Bridie nods. "Are you keeping well? Tell your mother I was asking after her."
w-bridie-greet-3: Bridie holds the door open a moment. "There's tea in the pot if you've a minute."
w-post-desc: The post office. Patrick is sweeping the step, humming to himself.
w-post-up: The post office sign, paint chipped. A swallow's nest under the eaves.
w-post-down: A coin face-down in a crack between the cobbles.
w-post-greet-1: "Good morning, good morning," says Patrick. "A fine day for the walking."
w-post-greet-2: Patrick leans on his brush. "You'll be needing stamps one of these days. Drop in."
w-post-greet-3: "Have you ever been to Boston?" Patrick begins, then catches himself. "Ah, another time."
w-school-desc: The schoolhouse. Children's voices from inside, doing their sums.
w-school-up: The bell above the gable, dark and still. A weathercock pointing east.
w-school-down: Worn flagstones, scuffed by fifty years of small feet.
w-school-greet-1: Mr Breen lifts his hand. "Good morning. It's good morning, isn't it? Yes. Yes, of course."
w-school-greet-2: Mr Breen nods slowly. "You've grown. Or maybe you haven't. The years are a tricky thing."
w-school-greet-3: Mr Breen smiles gently. "Off you go now. Mind the road."
w-brian-desc: Brian Blunt has come across the road with his clipboard.
w-brian-up: A line of cloud moving fast. Someone's washing on a line above the street.
w-brian-down: Cobbles, and a bit of straw blown from a hayrick.
w-brian-greet-1: Brian booms: "Did you see the match? Cone work, I'm telling you, it's the future."
w-brian-greet-2: Brian claps you on the shoulder. "Right so. Right so. On with it."
w-brian-greet-3: "Committee meeting Tuesday at eight sharp," says Brian. "Don't be late."
w-chapel-desc: The chapel. Bell quiet at this hour. Piano music drifts from inside.
w-chapel-up: The chapel spire, a single rook circling above.
w-chapel-down: Smooth flagstones polished by Sunday shoes.
w-chapel-greet-1: Mary tilts her head. "You walk on the balls of your feet. Did you know? It's good for you."
w-chapel-greet-2: "Are you musical?" Mary asks. "You should be. Everyone should be."
w-chapel-greet-3: Mary considers you. "There's a chord I've been working on. You'd like it. Maybe."
w-catherine-desc: Catherine O'Reilly's cottage. She's at her parlour window.
w-catherine-up: Ivy climbing the wall to the chimney. A wren darts in and out.
w-catherine-down: A border of flowers, weeded by old hands. A snail.
w-catherine-greet-1: Catherine taps the glass. "I saw you yesterday too. Are you keeping out of trouble?"
w-catherine-greet-2: "Lovely morning," Catherine calls through the glass. "Mind how you go on the lane."
w-catherine-greet-3: Catherine smiles. "Come in for tea. Or don't. It's a fine day either way."
w-doctor-desc: The well at the top of the lane. A blackbird singing somewhere in the holly. Dr O'Loughlin is passing by with his bag.
w-doctor-up: Holly leaves overhead, bright red berries. A bee among the late flowers.
w-doctor-down: The well's iron bucket, cold to look at. Moss between the stones.
w-doctor-greet-1: The doctor sets down his bag. "You're keeping well, I hope. No need to call me out today."
w-doctor-greet-2: "A walk is the best medicine," says the doctor. "And the cheapest."
w-doctor-greet-3: The doctor tips his hat. "I'll see you maybe never, please God."
w-pub-desc: The pub. Barney is at the door, wiping his hands on his apron.
w-pub-up: A weathered sign hanging from the bracket — The Crown, faded gold.
w-pub-down: Sawdust on the flagstones. A few stout-coloured rings.
w-pub-greet-1: "Will you be in tonight?" says Barney. "There's a fire on already."
w-pub-greet-2: "A grand morning altogether," says Barney. "Mind yourself on the road."
w-pub-greet-3: Barney nods. "Margaret's doing the books inside. I'll tell her you said hello."
w-james-desc: Back along the high street. Young James and his dog stop on the path.
w-james-up: Washing on a line strung between two windows. A pillowcase moving in the breeze.
w-james-down: Spot the dog sniffing at your shoes. Tail wagging cautiously.
w-james-greet-1: "His name is Spot," James says. "He's mostly good. He came back on his own this morning."
w-james-greet-2: James looks up, solemn as a judge. "It's a Saturday. I have to go to confession later. The dog doesn't."
w-james-greet-3: James waves carefully. "Good morning. The dog says good morning too. I think."
